title: Market Brief — Saturday, March 28, 2026
summary: B200 spot surged 15.2% overnight to $9.00/hr, signaling a demand spike for next-gen silicon even as H200s softened 5.2%. With TCO-adjusted B200 cost at $11.25/hr, anyone running inference on Blackwell at medium-tier pricing is hemorrhaging cash at -119% margins.
paragraphs: B200 spot surged 15.2% overnight to $9.00/hr, signaling a demand spike for next-gen silicon even as H200s softened 5.2%. With TCO-adjusted B200 cost at $11.25/hr, anyone running inference on Blackwell at medium-tier pricing is hemorrhaging cash at -119% margins.

DeepSeek V3.2 sells output at $0.38/M against a Llama 70B TCO-adjusted production cost north of $5.50/M on H100s, meaning DeepSeek is burning cash at roughly $5/M tokens to buy market share. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output is the lone fat margin play, while Anthropic grades Neutral with Opus 4.6 commanding $25/M output but Haiku 4.5 squeezed at $5/M. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

H200 at $2.32/hr spot ($2.90 TCO) is undervalued relative to H100 at $1.55/hr ($1.94 TCO) given superior inference throughput per dollar. Rotate out of B200 spot exposure at $9.00/hr until availability clears 40% and pricing normalizes below $6.
bullets: 21.41 LLMflation index means API prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launched at $60/M output in March 2023.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 is 210x cheaper than OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output.
22% H200 availability is the tightest across all three GPU classes tracked today.
$9.00/hr B200 median spot is 3x the floor price of $3.00/hr, showing extreme bid dispersion.
97% margin at ultra-premium tier on H100 means top-tier API providers net roughly $139/M tokens above cost.
0.38 index score for DeepSeek and open-source combined is 66x below Anthropic's 25, reflecting aggressive below-cost pricing.
